spec.reasoning: The task has a risk flag 'task_depends_on_blank_row_positions', but this task is about finding missing dates from a sequence in column A and placing them in column C — it does not delete or insert rows as part of its operation, so title_row_insert is safe. The title row insert shifts the header from row 1 to row 2 and all data down by one row, which tests whether the model hardcodes row positions (e.g., assuming header is always row 1). The answer position C2:C13 will shift to C3:C14 accordingly. I did not rename 'Date' because the word 'dates' appears naturally in the instruction body as a general English word, not purely as a column reference — renaming it would risk confusion per Rule 6. The column header 'Column B lists all missing dates' also contains the word 'dates' used in the instruction. A distractor column 'Date Difference' is added after the last column to test whether the model is distracted by a plausibly related but irrelevant column.
